name: sem11_key_equation_importance_eval
description: Batch 16 eval cases for key-equation semantic importance.

input.latex: \documentclass{article}\begin{document}\section{Setup}We repeatedly use Eq.~\eqref{eq:def}. Eq.~\eqref{eq:def} fixes notation.\begin{equation}\label{eq:def} D(x)=x^2 \end{equation}\section{Results}Our central result is the following relation controlling the final spectrum.\begin{equation}\label{eq:result} M^2 = g^2 + m_0^2 \end{equation}This is the main result of the paper.\end{document}

evaluations.reason: Defines notation but is not the central contribution.
Explicitly framed as the paper's main result.

input.latex: \documentclass{article}\begin{document}\section{Formalism}The amplitude below is standard setup notation.\begin{equation}\label{eq:amp} \mathcal{A}(s,t)=g^2 f(s,t) \end{equation}\section{Phenomenology}The observable prediction relevant for comparison is\begin{equation}\label{eq:obs} R = \frac{\sigma_{\rm signal}}{\sigma_{\rm bg}} \end{equation}We use Eq.~\eqref{eq:obs} as the final prediction and central observable.\end{document}

evaluations.reason: Interesting formalism signal, but not the paper's main reported result.
Presented as the final prediction for comparison.

input.latex: \documentclass{article}\begin{document}\section{Model}We define $\lambda_{\rm eff}=g^2/m^2$ for convenience.\section{Conclusion}The actual take-away equation is\begin{equation}\label{eq:final} \Delta E = \lambda_{\rm eff} T^4 \end{equation}This expression summarizes the final scaling law.\end{document}

evaluations.reason: This is the final reported scaling relation.
